_meta.source: FMP MCP connector (quote endpoint for VIX; premarket/news scan for flags)
_meta.note: Companion file for the dip/pop OHLCV feed (dip_pop_ohlcv.csv). Overwritten each run (not appended) - always reflects the latest snapshot, refreshed at premarket and market-close task runs. Flag values: Normal | Caution | Avoid. Avoid should gate a green technical verdict; Caution should annotate it.
_meta.category_note: category = the primary driver behind the flag: macro-scheduled | sector-contagion | single-name | geopolitical | other. See dip_pop_flag_history.csv for the append-only burn-in log.

flags.reason: MU -8.0% ($904.28) in a memory-complex rout triggered by CXMT's $8.6-9.8B Shanghai STAR IPO (book-building opened Jul 15, lists Jul 27 - Bloomberg/Reuters, Jul 14-15); China's #1 DRAM maker (Q1 rev +719% YoY) is a structural competitive threat; cooler June PPI (-0.3% vs flat exp, BLS Jul 15) removed macro pressure but didn't stem the sector selloff

SNDK -8.1% ($1,615) in the CXMT-IPO memory rout plus an Argus coverage initiation without a buy rating (Motley Fool, Jul 15); beta 4.74 - intraday range $1,478.50-$1,729.50 (14.5% swing); still ~2% above T2 ceiling $1,581

MSFT +2.8% ($395.63) on cooler June PPI + dovish Beige Book (price growth same/slower in all districts - Fed, Jul 15); no negative catalyst; note session high $398.96 pierced the $396 range top intraday - range-trade assumption on watch
